x eslint(no-useless-escape): Unnecessary escape character 'g'
    ,-[_fixtures/react/jsx.jsx:18:35]
 17 |     {greeting.endsWith(",")
 18 |     ? " " : <span style={{color: '\grey'}}>", "</span> }
    :                                   ^^
 19 |     <em>
    `----
  help: Replace `\g` with `g`.

  x eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y(mouse-events-have-key-events): `onMouseOver` must be accompanied by `onFocus` for accessibility.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/jsx.jsx:15:80]
 14 | 
 15 |   return <div className='HelloWorld' title={`You are visitor number ${ num }`} onMouseOver={onMouseOver}>
    :                                                                                ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
 16 |     <strong>{ greeting.slice( 0, 1 ).toUpperCase() + greeting.slice(1).toLowerCase() }</strong>
    `----
  help: Try to add `onFocus`.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
   ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:2:11]
 1 | export function Component1() {
 2 |   return <div/>;
   :           ^^^
 3 | }
   `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
   ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:8:12]
 7 |   b:2}
 8 |   return < a  foo= 'bar' bar={`foo` } >
   :            ^
 9 |      <div {...props }
   `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:9:7]
  8 |   return < a  foo= 'bar' bar={`foo` } >
  9 |      <div {...props }
    :       ^^^
 10 |      a={1}
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:12:6]
 11 |     b="2">Inline Text</div>
 12 |     <Component1>
    :      ^^^^^^^^^^
 13 |       Block Text
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:15:8]
 14 |       </Component1>
 15 |       <div>
    :        ^^^
 16 |         Mixed
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:17:14]
 16 |         Mixed
 17 |             <div>Foo</div>
    :              ^^^
 18 |           Text<b > Bar</b>
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:18:16]
 17 |             <div>Foo</div>
 18 |           Text<b > Bar</b>
    :                ^
 19 |         </div>
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:20:10]
 19 |         </div>
 20 |         <p>
    :          ^
 21 |           foo<i>bar</i><b>baz</b>
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:21:15]
 20 |         <p>
 21 |           foo<i>bar</i><b>baz</b>
    :               ^
 22 |         </p>
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  ! eslint-plugin-react(react-in-jsx-scope): `React` must be in scope when using JSX.
    ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:21:25]
 20 |         <p>
 21 |           foo<i>bar</i><b>baz</b>
    :                         ^
 22 |         </p>
    `----
  help: When using JSX, `<a />` expands to `React.createElement("a")`. Therefore the `React` variable must be in scope.

  x eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y(anchor-is-valid): Missing `href` attribute for the `a` element.
   ,-[_fixtures/react/tsx.tsx:8:12]
 7 |   b:2}
 8 |   return < a  foo= 'bar' bar={`foo` } >
   :            ^
 9 |      <div {...props }
   `----
  help: Provide the `href` attribute for the `a` element.

Found 10 warnings and 3 errors.
